text: Meeting deadlines is a crucial skill in both academic and professional settings. Good time management starts with breaking a large project into smaller tasks and setting mini-deadlines for each. Prioritization helps you focus on the most urgent or important items first, ensuring that critical work is completed on time. Scope management involves clearly defining what the project includes and avoiding unnecessary additions that can delay progress. Finally, open communication with your team or supervisor is essential. If you foresee a problem, inform others early so adjustments can be made. By combining these strategies, you can handle deadlines more effectively and reduce stress.
